definitions: A set of technology-based solutions that optimize the management of working capital and liquidity within a supply chain.
Financial solutions that facilitate the flow of funds and information between buyers and suppliers in a supply chain.
A broad term for solutions that improve cash flow for businesses by leveraging the creditworthiness of larger buyers to offer early payment options to their suppliers.
examples: Supply chain finance can benefit both large corporations and small businesses.
Many companies are exploring supply chain finance to improve their working capital.
The implementation of supply chain finance requires robust technological platforms.
